name: Arditurri Mines Trail
name_original: Minas de Arditurri (PR-GI 1009) — circular por el Parque Natural Aiako Harria
description: Hike the moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) Minas de Arditurri (PR-GI 1009) loop in Aiako Harria Natural Park, exploring ancient mines and diverse l
region_intro: Hiking around Oiartzun offers diverse landscapes within the Basque Country's Gipuzkoa province. The region is characterized by the rugged granite formations of Aiako Harria Natural Park, ancient beech forests, and the meandering River Oiartzun. Trails often provide views of the Bay of Biscay and the surrounding mountainous terrain. This area combines natural features with historical sites, including ancient mining reserves and megalithic structures. Best hiking trails around Oiartzun The most popular hiking route is Arditurri Mines Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate path leads through the historic Arditurri Mining Reserve, offering a blend of nature and industrial heritage. Another top favourite among local hikers is Basate y Munagirre — circular through Aiako Harria Natural Park, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This route explores the natural park's varied terrain, including its distinctive beech forests. Local hikers also love the Alto de Aritxulegi from Minas de Arditurri — circular by the Peñas de Aya, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through the challenging Peñas de Aya, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Oiartzun is defined by dramatic granite mountains, ancient beech forests, and coastal views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Oiartzun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 5900 hikers have used komoot to explore Oiartzun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Oiartzun?
A: Oiartzun offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 85 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from leisurely strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Oiartzun?
A: The trails around Oiartzun offer a good mix of difficulties. You'll find approximately 15 easy routes, 49 moderate paths, and 21 more challenging trails, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Oiartzun?
A: The hiking routes in Oiartzun are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 760 reviews. More than 5,900 hikers have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Oiartzun?
A: Yes, Oiartzun has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047291709/arditurri-mines-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arditurri Mines Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, offering a blend of nature and industrial heritage along a former railway line. For even easier options, look for routes marked as 'easy' on komoot.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Oiartzun?
A: Many of Oiartzun's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047350316/basate-y-munagirre-circular-through-aiako-harria-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basate y Munagirre — circular through Aiako Harria Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the park's varied terrain and distinctive beech forests.

Q: Are there trails that offer historical or cultural points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Oiartzun is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047291709/arditurri-mines-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arditurri Mines Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the Arditurri Mining Reserve, where you can explore ancient mines. Additionally, within Aiako Harria Natural Park, you can discover the Oiartzun Megalithic Site with its impressive cromlechs and burial sites.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls or significant natural monuments?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful natural sights. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzondo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; near some of the hiking routes. The area also boasts natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/189561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Ataloi&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of Colors&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which trails offer the best views of the coast or mountains?
A: Many routes in Oiartzun provide stunning vistas. Trails within the Aiako Harria Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351620681/alto-de-aritxulegi-from-minas-de-arditurri-circular-by-the-penas-de-aya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Aritxulegi from Minas de Arditurri — circular by the Peñas de Aya&lt;/a&gt;, offer panoramic views of the Southern Basque Country. Some trails also provide glimpses of the Bay of Biscay and the Basque coast.

Q: Are there any trails that go through unique forests?
A: Oiartzun is renowned for its mysterious beech forests, particularly within Aiako Harria Natural Park. These forests, characterized by their unique, 'hauntingly beautiful' appearance due to ancient charcoal production methods, can be explored on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351845353/heart-of-aiako-harria-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Heart of Aiako Harria Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, several trails lead to or offer views of beautiful water bodies. You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5157946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Xoldokogaina&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/994862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Endara-San Anton Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity, with some routes passing close to them.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking options in Oiartzun?
A: While specific restrictions can vary by natural park or private land, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Oiartzun region, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park regulations for the specific trail you plan to hike.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Oiartzun?
A: The spring and autumn months typically offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking in Oiartzun, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring cooler, wetter weather, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there options for public transport to reach hiking trailheads in Oiartzun?
A: Oiartzun is part of the public transport network in Gipuzkoa. While some trailheads might be directly accessible by bus, others may require a short walk from the nearest stop. It's recommended to check local bus schedules and routes to plan your access to specific hiking areas.
tips: Arditurri mining preserve, an old mining complex located in Las Peñas de Aya. This mining complex began to be exploited before the arrival of the Roman Empire and it remained active, practically without interruption, until 1984. Silver (argentiferous galena), iron, lead, zinc, fluorite and blende have been obtained from it. In 2004 it became part of the Peñas de Aya Natural Park and, after the 800-meter gallery was set up, it is open to the public for their visit and understanding.
Small and pretty waterfall located half way from the Arditurri mines.

The Greenway crosses the entire municipality and ends at the foot of the imposing Aiako Harria and runs parallel to the Oiartzun river, on the old railway line that linked the Arditurri mines – exploited since the 1st century by the Romans – with the port of Pasaia .

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Minas de Arditurri (PR-GI 1009) trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike usually takes around 2 hours and 22 minutes to complete. However, if you plan to explore the mining complex or take breaks, allow for more time.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The PR-GI 1009 is rated as a moderate hike. This means it requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness due to varied terrain, though the paths are generally accessible.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions or points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a rich blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll start at the historic Arditurri Mining Complex, which dates back to the Roman Empire. Along the way, you can also see a beautiful Waterfall on the Arditurri Mines Trail and walk along a Section of the Arditurri Greenway, an old railway line converted into a path.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Minas de Arditurri (PR-GI 1009) trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, plunging into the heart of the Aiako Harria Natural Park. You'll encounter unpaved paths, remnants of mining activities, serene ponds, streams, and dense oak and beech woods. Sure-footedness is required, as some surfaces can be uneven.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Aiako Harria Natural Park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant foliage. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Aiako Harria Natural Park or the Arditurri mines?
faqs.answer: There are no general entrance fees or permits required to hike the PR-GI 1009 trail within the Aiako Harria Natural Park. However, if you wish to take a guided tour inside the Arditurri Mining Complex, there will be a separate fee for that experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Minas de Arditurri (PR-GI 1009) circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Arditurri Mining Complex, where parking facilities are available. It's advisable to arrive earlier during peak seasons to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I see in Aiako Harria Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The park is home to diverse wildlife, including European mink, desman, salmon, peregrine falcons, and griffon vultures. The landscape is characterized by dense oak and beech woods, along with granite peaks and rugged gorges.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route extensively follows the "Arditurriko meategiak" trail (100% of the route) and also includes significant sections of the "Arditurriko bidegorria" (Arditurri Greenway), which makes up about 26% of the route. You'll also find smaller sections intersecting with "[CIMA NA04] Agiña * Oiartzun" and "Tornola errekako iturburua".

faqs.question: Is it possible to explore the Arditurri mines themselves?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Arditurri mines offer a unique opportunity to explore some of the underground galleries through guided tours. These tours often incorporate sound and light effects, providing an immersive journey into the mines' 2,000-year history.

waypoints.name: Waterfall on the Arditurri Mines Trail
